title: Support Multi-Cluster name resolution with Consul
body:
<!-- If you need to report a security issue please visit https://docs.dapr.io/operations/support/support-security-issues -->

## Describe the feature
<!-- Please also discuss possible business value -->

For name resolution across multiple connected clusters, Consul should be able to resolve Dapr services across clusters. This will enable service invocation across Dapr services distributed across multiple services

This is in alignment with the proposal - https://github.com/dapr/dapr/issues/5389
## Release Note
<!-- How should this new feature be announced in our release notes? It can be populated later. -->
<!-- Keep it as a single line. Examples: -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **ADD** New feature in Dapr. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **FIX** Bug in runtime. -->
<!-- RELEASE NOTE: **UPDATE** Runtime dependency. -->

RELEASE NOTE:
**ADD** Multi-Cluster service invocation with Consul building block

comments.body:
Can you define "connected clusters"?

No objection to the name resolution across cluster, but I don't think this addresses service invocation.

Please do outline how clusters must be connected for this to work.

> Can you define "connected clusters"?
Cluster -> compute/data/network resources managed by a common control plane [ one or more depending on HA] Kubernetes 
Connected Cluster -> clusters that are setup to communicate to exchange control plane information and allow data plane traffic to flow [ achieved via network reachability, cert management, policy configuration etc]
 
> No objection to the name resolution across cluster, but I don't think this addresses service invocation.
> 
> Please do outline how clusters must be connected for this to work.
Will add doc to describe setup or link to appropriate resources on Consul documentation
